generated_oml:
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor capturing raw point cloud data for object detection in ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RawLiDARData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensors monitoring vehicle speed, position, and other state parameters for ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance RawLiDARData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ProjectedPathData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor, LiDARSensor
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks derived from LiDAR point cloud data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringEnabler
]

instance TranslatedVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state data serialized and converted for cross-component communication"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance TranslatedProjectedPathData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Projected driving path data after serialization and unit conversion"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProjectedPathData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance PointCloudClusteringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component for clustering and tracking objects from LiDAR point cloud data"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Ensures data integrity through serialization, message type alignment, and unit conversion across all communication streams"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance CloudComputeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud-based resource for offloading safety-critical ACC computations"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ThreeDVisualizationUI : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization component for real-time monitoring of vehicle and object data"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ControlPanelUI : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "User interface for remote control of ACC system parameters"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Non-standardized data storage component for logging and report generation"
    DTDFVocab:enables ReportGeneration
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state, object tracks, and driving path with reporting capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateVisualization, ObjectTracksVisualization, DrivingPathVisualization, ReportGeneration
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC-related computations (lead vehicle detection, speed calculation, actuation) from ECUs to cloud resources"
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDetection, SpeedComputation, ActuationCommandGeneration
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables remote control of ACC system via UI, including enable/disable, speed setting, and emergency braking"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCEnableDisable, DesiredSpeedSetting, EmergencyBrakingCommand
]

instance VehicleStateVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle speed, position, and other state parameters"
]

instance ObjectTracksVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time display of detected objects from LiDAR processing"
]

instance DrivingPathVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of projected driving path based on ACC system"
]

instance ReportGeneration : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Ability to save and generate reports from monitored data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance LeadVehicleDetection : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of lead vehicles for ACC system"
]

instance SpeedComputation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Calculation of optimal vehicle speed based on distance to lead vehicle"
]

instance ActuationCommandGeneration : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generation of acceleration/braking commands for vehicle control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCEnableDisable : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote activation or deactivation of ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DesiredSpeedSetting : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Setting of desired speed for ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingCommand : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Immediate braking command issued remotely in emergency situations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC), including speed regulation and steering angle adjustments, are generated by the ACC Controlling FE and transmitted via ROS2 network with Data Translation handling serialization and message alignment. Actuation FE components manage motor and steering servo actuation, though specific validation protocols, authentication mechanisms, feedback systems, conflict resolution strategies, and command execution confirmations are not documented."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Requires 100 ms clock synchronization accuracy for computation offloading between physical and digital components in safety-critical vehicular applications, constrained by hardware limitations despite industry standards typically mandating stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The architecture follows ISO 23247 RA with three entities (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User) deployed on separate hardware. Functional Entities are implemented as ROS2 packages with ROS2 nodes. Physical Twin Entity's design departs from ISO 23247 by hosting general automotive functionality (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) and reflecting inherent system resources, impacting functionality ownership and engineering processes. Coordination relies on ROS2 communication and 100 ms clock synchronization for safety-critical vehicular applications."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Communication between software components occurs within a ROS2 network, while inter-entity communication occurs over a Wi-Fi network using UDP in the transport layer. Network topology, bandwidth requirements, latency specifications, reliability mechanisms, and security protocols are not provided in the document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the ISO 23247 standard, which includes four parts: Overview and general principles, Reference architecture, Digital representation of physical manufacturing elements, and Information exchange. The reference architecture (Part 2) defines an entity-based structure comprising Observable Manufacturing Elements (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity. Functional Entities (FEs) implement specific tasks such as data transformation and user interfacing, with OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) cited as a protocol example for implementation."
]
